# Inertia

> Declarative animation primitives for React Native, built on react-native-reanimated. Inspired by Framer Motion (web) and react-spring (cross-platform). DX-first ŌĆö animations are props on a component, not imperative shared values, worklets, and `useAnimatedStyle` boilerplate.

## Install

```sh
pnpm add @onlynative/inertia react-native-reanimated
```

Enable the Reanimated Babel plugin per its install guide.

## Imports

```ts
import {
  Motion,
  Presence,
  MotionConfig,
  useVariants,
  useMotionValue,
  useSpring,
  useTransform,
  useScroll,
} from '@onlynative/inertia'
// or for tree-shaking:
import { MotionView } from '@onlynative/inertia/view'
import { MotionText } from '@onlynative/inertia/text'
import { MotionImage } from '@onlynative/inertia/image'
import { MotionPressable } from '@onlynative/inertia/pressable'
import { MotionScrollView } from '@onlynative/inertia/scroll-view'
```

## Public API

- `Motion.View` / `Motion.Text` / `Motion.Image` / `Motion.Pressable` / `Motion.ScrollView` ŌĆö animatable primitives. Per-primitive style inference (no shared `ViewStyle & TextStyle & ImageStyle` fallback).
- `<Presence>` ŌĆö mount / unmount transitions; children need explicit `key`s. Exiting children get `pointerEvents: 'none'` automatically.
- `<MotionConfig reducedMotion="user" | "never" | "always">` ŌĆö gates motion against the OS reduce-motion setting (default `"user"`).
- `useVariants(variants, initial?)` ŌĆö returns `{ current, transitionTo }` controller for the `controller` prop.
- `useMotionValue(initial)` ŌĆö thin pass-through over `useSharedValue<T>`. Returns a `SharedValue<T>` directly so it interops with every Reanimated API without unwrapping.
- `useSpring(target, config?)` ŌĆö animate a `SharedValue<number>` toward `target` with react-spring vocab. `target` may be a plain number (effect-driven) or a `SharedValue<number>` (UI-thread reaction).
- `useTransform(value, inputRange, outputRange, options?)` / `useTransform(transformer)` ŌĆö interpolate a numeric shared value onto a number or color range, or derive any value from any number of shared values via a worklet. Non-worklet transformers are auto-wrapped.
- `useScroll()` ŌĆö returns `{ scrollX, scrollY, onScroll }` for use with `Motion.ScrollView`. Scroll events fire on the UI thread.
- `createMotionComponent<C>(C)` ŌĆö wrap any component with the same Motion prop surface, inferring style from `C`.

## Motion props

`initial` (mount-only, non-reactive after first render ŌĆö pass `false` to skip), `animate`, `exit`, `variants`, `controller`, `gesture`, `transition`, `onAnimationEnd`.

## Transitions

| `type`           | Public config                                                    | Default? |
| ---------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- |
| `'spring'`       | `tension`, `friction`, `mass`, `velocity`, `delay`, `repeat`     | yes      |
| `'timing'`       | `duration`, `easing`, `delay`, `repeat`                          |          |
| `'decay'`        | `velocity`, `deceleration`, `clamp`, `delay`                     |          |
| `'no-animation'` | ŌĆö                                                                |          |

Spring uses react-spring vocabulary (`tension`, `friction`, `mass`). Reanimated's raw `stiffness` / `damping` never appear in the public API. Per-property `transition` entries override the top-level config.

## Sequences and repeat

```tsx
<Motion.View
  animate={{
    translateX: [0, 100, 0],
    opacity: [0, { to: 1, type: 'spring' }, { to: 0, delay: 500 }],
  }}
  transition={{ repeat: 'infinite' }}
/>
```

`repeat`: `number` (finite, alternating) | `'infinite'` | `{ count, alternate }`.

## Gestures

```tsx
<Motion.View
  gesture={{
    hovered: { backgroundColor: '#0001' },
    focused: { backgroundColor: '#0002' },
    pressed: { backgroundColor: '#0003' },
  }}
  transition={{ type: 'timing', duration: 120 }}
/>
```

Sub-states layer **additively** in priority order: `hovered ŌåÆ focused ŌåÆ focusVisible ŌåÆ pressed`. Each declared sub-state owns an independent progress (0Ōåö1) that fades in/out with its own transition; the worklet composites layers via `lerp` (numerics) / `interpolateColor` (colors). MD3 release-while-hovered behaves correctly ŌĆö the press layer fades out without disturbing the hover layer.

Per-layer transitions via `transition.<stateName>`:

```tsx
transition={{
  pressed:  { type: 'timing', duration: 50 },
  hovered:  { type: 'timing', duration: 90 },
}}
```

`focusVisible` engages only on keyboard focus (W3C `:focus-visible`) so click-focus on web doesn't flash a ring; on native it tracks `focused`. `hovered` is a no-op on native.

## Caveats

- `Motion.Pressable` does **not** support function-form `style={({ pressed }) => ...}`. Reanimated's animated-component wrapper silently drops it. Drive press/focus/hover styling through `gesture` instead, or compute conditional styles once in render.
- `initial` is read once on mount and intentionally non-reactive. To reset after a state change, change the component `key`, remount via `<Presence>`, or drive the value through a controller. Pass `initial={false}` to skip the initial-mount animation.

## Animatable properties (alpha)

Numeric: `opacity`, `translateX`, `translateY`, `scale`, `scaleX`, `scaleY`, `rotate`, `width`, `height`, `borderRadius`.

Color: `backgroundColor`, `borderColor`, `color`, `tintColor` (Image only). Hex, `rgb()` / `rgba()`, `hsl()` / `hsla()`, and named colors all work; the target string is forwarded straight through `withSpring` / `withTiming` and Reanimated handles RGBA interpolation natively.

Layout (`layout` / `layoutId`) and SVG path morphing are not in alpha.

## Docs

- Full docs: https://onlynative.github.io/inertia/
- Per-page LLM reference: https://onlynative.github.io/inertia/llms-full.txt
- Source: https://github.com/onlynative/inertia
